issue_title: When I close a session, I get the message...
issue_body:
### What happened?

When I close a session, I get the message "To resume this session: gemini --resume 'bla bla bla bla'", which is incorrect because it produces the error "Error resuming session: Invalid session identifier "'bla bla bla'". To solve this, you need to remove the parentheses. Please fix this issue so that the message is written without the parentheses when closing a session, or make it work with the parentheses. I won't be returning to this forum anymore, so don't ask any questions.

### What did you expect to happen?

Problem Solving

### Client information

CLI Version                              0.41.2
Git Commit                               b0c7a1722
Model                                    Auto (Gemini 3)
Sandbox                                  no sandbox
OS                                       win32
Auth Method                              Signed in with Google
Tier                                     Gemini Code Assist in Google One AI Pro

### Login information

_No response_

### Anything else we need to know?

_No response_
notes: This PR was not actually merged, but it still looked like a good example.

summary.problem: When closing a session, the resume session message is printed with unnecessary quoting on Windows/PowerShell (or resolveSession fails to parse session IDs wrapped in quotes), preventing users from copy-pasting the resume command on Windows.
summary.root_cause: The SessionSummaryDisplay component wraps the session ID in redundant double quotes on Windows (or resolveSession does not strip enclosing quotes from command line arguments passed literally on Windows CMD).
summary.context: Removing the redundant quote wrapping in the UI, or making the resolver quote-tolerant, resolves the issue.

steps: Either: Remove the conditional isWindows double-quote wrapping in SessionSummaryDisplay.tsx and output the escaped session ID directly.
Or: Update resolveSession inside packages/cli/src/utils/sessionUtils.ts to detect and strip matching enclosing single or double quotes from the input argument.

testing_strategy.expected_behavior: The printed resume command displays without redundant quotes on Windows, or resolveSession successfully loads a session ID enclosed in single or double quotes.
verification_steps: Either: Assert that SessionSummaryDisplay renders the resume command without surrounding double quotes on Windows.
Or: Add a unit test in packages/cli/src/utils/sessionUtils.test.ts verifying that resolveSession successfully loads a session ID wrapped in single or double quotes.

golden_spec_rationale: The human PR modified SessionSummaryDisplay.tsx to remove quotes in printed output. However, modifying resolveSession() in packages/cli/src/utils/sessionUtils.ts is a valid, more robust input-parsing fix that strips quotes from arguments (needed when copy-pasting on Windows CMD). Both approaches fully solve the defect, so the golden spec accepts either file location.
